// Caso: Promedio (clásico) | Nivel: Básico | Categoría: Clásicos
function solve(arr)
  if #arr==0 then return nil end
  local s=0
  for _,x in ipairs(arr) do s=s+x end
  return s / #arr
end

snippets.C0646:
// Caso: Validar RUT chileno | Nivel: Intermedio | Categoría: Validación
function solve(rut)
  local clean = tostring(rut):gsub("[%.%-%s]", ""):upper()
  if #clean < 2 then return false end

  local body = clean:sub(1, #clean-1)
  local dv = clean:sub(#clean, #clean)

  if not body:match("^%d+$") then return false end

  local sum = 0
  local mul = 2
  for i = #body, 1, -1 do
    sum = sum + tonumber(body:sub(i,i)) * mul
    mul = (mul == 7) and 2 or (mul + 1)
  end

  local mod = 11 - (sum % 11)
  local dvCalc = (mod == 11) and "0" or (mod == 10) and "K" or tostring(mod)
  return dv == dvCalc
end

snippets.C0650:
-- C0650 · Leer/Escribir CSV (UTF-8) (Intermedio, IO)
-- Lua: CSV simple (sin comillas/escapes avanzados).
local function split(line)
  local out = {}
  for part in string.gmatch(line, "([^,]+)") do
    out[#out+1] = part:gsub("[\r\n]+","")
  end
  return out
end

local function lower(s) return string.lower((s or ""):gsub("^%s+",""):gsub("%s+$","")) end

function solve()
  local f = io.open("input.csv","r"); if not f then return end
  local out = io.open("output.csv","w"); if not out then f:close(); return end

  local header = f:read("*l"); if not header then f:close(); out:close(); return end
  out:write(header, "\n")

  local headers = split(header)
  local emailIdx = -1
  for i=1,#headers do if headers[i] == "Email" then emailIdx = i end end

  for line in f:lines() do
    local cols = split(line)
    if emailIdx ~= -1 and cols[emailIdx] then cols[emailIdx] = lower(cols[emailIdx]) end
    out:write(table.concat(cols, ","), "\n")
  end

  f:close(); out:close()
end
snippets.C0651:
-- C0651 · Consumir API HTTP (GET y POST JSON) (Intermedio, HTTP)
-- Lua: requiere LuaSocket
-- luarocks install luasocket
local http = require("socket.http")
local ltn12 = require("ltn12")

function solve()
  -- GET
  local getBody = {}
  http.request{ url="https://httpbin.org/get", sink=ltn12.sink.table(getBody) }
  local getStr = table.concat(getBody)

  -- POST JSON
  local postBody = {}
  local payload = '{"hello":"world"}'
  http.request{
    url="https://httpbin.org/post",
    method="POST",
    headers={["Content-Type"]="application/json", ["Content-Length"]=tostring(#payload)},
    source=ltn12.source.string(payload),
    sink=ltn12.sink.table(postBody)
  }
  local postStr = table.concat(postBody)

  return { get = getStr, post = postStr } -- parse JSON con dkjson/cjson si quieres
end

snippets.C0661:
-- C0661 · Retry + backoff + timeout (Intermedio, Resiliencia)
-- Lua: retry con backoff (sleep requiere socket.sleep o similar)
-- luarocks install luasocket
local socket = require("socket")

function solve(maxRetries)
  maxRetries = maxRetries or 5
  local attempt = 0
  local delay = 0.2
  while true do
    attempt = attempt + 1
    if math.random() >= 0.7 then return true end
    if attempt >= maxRetries then return false end
    socket.sleep(delay)
    delay = math.min(delay * 2, 5.0)
  end
end

snippets.C0665:
-- C0665 · Binary search (iterativo) (Básico, Algoritmos)
function solve(arr, target)
  local lo = 1
  local hi = #arr
  while lo <= hi do
    local mid = math.floor((lo + hi) / 2)
    local v = arr[mid]
    if v == target then return mid end
    if v < target then lo = mid + 1 else hi = mid - 1 end
  end
  return -1
end
snippets.C0666:
-- C0666 · Merge sort (Intermedio, Algoritmos)
local function merge(left, right)
  local out = {}
  local i, j = 1, 1
  while i <= #left and j <= #right do
    if left[i] <= right[j] then out[#out+1] = left[i]; i = i + 1
    else out[#out+1] = right[j]; j = j + 1 end
  end
  while i <= #left do out[#out+1] = left[i]; i = i + 1 end
  while j <= #right do out[#out+1] = right[j]; j = j + 1 end
  return out
end

function solve(arr)
  if #arr <= 1 then return arr end
  local mid = math.floor(#arr / 2)
  local left = {}
  local right = {}
  for i=1,mid do left[#left+1]=arr[i] end
  for i=mid+1,#arr do right[#right+1]=arr[i] end
  left = solve(left)
  right = solve(right)
  return merge(left, right)
end
snippets.C0667:
-- C0667 · BFS/DFS (grafo) (Intermedio, Algoritmos)
-- graph: table adjacency list, nodes can be numbers or strings.
function bfs(graph, start)
  local q = {start}
  local head = 1
  local seen = {[start] = true}
  local out = {}

  while head <= #q do
    local v = q[head]; head = head + 1
    out[#out+1] = v
    local neigh = graph[v] or {}
    for i=1,#neigh do
      local n = neigh[i]
      if not seen[n] then
        seen[n] = true
        q[#q+1] = n
      end
    end
  end
  return out
end

local function dfs_visit(graph, v, seen, out)
  seen[v] = true
  out[#out+1] = v
  local neigh = graph[v] or {}
  for i=1,#neigh do
    local n = neigh[i]
    if not seen[n] then dfs_visit(graph, n, seen, out) end
  end
end

function dfs(graph, start)
  local seen = {}
  local out = {}
  dfs_visit(graph, start, seen, out)
  return out
end
snippets.C0668:
-- C0668 · A* (pathfinding) (Avanzado, Algoritmos)
local function hcost(a,b)
  return math.abs(a[1]-b[1]) + math.abs(a[2]-b[2])
end

local function key(p) return p[1] .. "," .. p[2] end

local function neighbors(grid, p)
  local w = #grid[1]
  local h = #grid
  local x,y = p[1], p[2]
  local out = {}
  local dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}
  for i=1,#dirs do
    local nx = x + dirs[i][1]
    local ny = y + dirs[i][2]
    if nx>=1 and ny>=1 and nx<=w and ny<=h and grid[ny][nx]==0 then
      out[#out+1] = {nx, ny}
    end
  end
  return out
end

local function reconstruct(came, cur)
  local path = {cur}
  local ck = key(cur)
  while came[ck] do
    cur = came[ck]
    table.insert(path, cur)
    ck = key(cur)
  end
  -- reverse
  for i=1, math.floor(#path/2) do
    path[i], path[#path-i+1] = path[#path-i+1], path[i]
  end
  return path
end

function solve(grid, start, goal)
  local open = { {hcost(start,goal), start} }
  local came = {}
  local g = { [key(start)] = 0 }

  while #open > 0 do
    table.sort(open, function(a,b) return a[1] < b[1] end)
    local cur = table.remove(open, 1)[2]
    if cur[1]==goal[1] and cur[2]==goal[2] then
      return reconstruct(came, cur)
    end
    for _, nb in ipairs(neighbors(grid, cur)) do
      local nk = key(nb)
      local ng = g[key(cur)] + 1
      if g[nk] == nil or ng < g[nk] then
        g[nk] = ng
        came[nk] = cur
        open[#open+1] = { ng + hcost(nb,goal), nb }
      end
    end
  end
  return {}
end
snippets.C0669:
-- C0669 · LRU cache (Intermedio, Algoritmos)
-- Lua: implementación simple con tabla + lista (O(n) en touch)
LRU = {}
LRU.__index = LRU

function LRU.new(cap)
  return setmetatable({cap=cap, map={}, order={}}, LRU)
end

local function indexOf(t, v)
  for i=1,#t do if t[i]==v then return i end end
  return nil
end

function LRU:get(k)
  local v = self.map[k]
  if v == nil then return nil end
  local idx = indexOf(self.order, k)
  if idx then table.remove(self.order, idx) end
  table.insert(self.order, k)
  return v
end

function LRU:put(k, v)
  self.map[k] = v
  local idx = indexOf(self.order, k)
  if idx then table.remove(self.order, idx) end
  table.insert(self.order, k)
  if #self.order > self.cap then
    local oldest = table.remove(self.order, 1)
    self.map[oldest] = nil
  end
end

snippets.C0680:
// C0680 · Uso de teclado (keydown/atajos) (Intermedio, UI/Entrada)
-- Lua: stdin (Enter)
function solve()
  print("Escribe una tecla y Enter (w/a/s/d, q):")
  while true do
    local line = io.read()
    if not line then break end
    if #line == 0 then goto continue end
    local k = string.lower(string.sub(line,1,1))
    if k == "q" then break end
    if k == "w" then print("Mover: arriba") end
    if k == "s" then print("Mover: abajo") end
    if k == "a" then print("Mover: izquierda") end
    if k == "d" then print("Mover: derecha") end
    ::continue::
  end
end

-- C0682 · Despliegue de hora activa (Básico, UI/Tiempo)
-- reloj en consola (sleep requiere luasocket)
local socket = require("socket")

function solve()
  while true do
    io.write("\r" .. os.date("%H:%M:%S"))
    io.flush()
    socket.sleep(1)
  end
end
